label: 46306 Registration violations involving aircraft not providing air transportation

L. 100–690, § 7209(a)102 Stat. 4429In subsections (b)(9), (d), and (e), the word “Administrator” in section 902(b) of the Federal Aviation Act of 1958 (, ) is retained on authority of 49:106(g).Public Law 85–72672 Stat. 784In subsection (b), before clause (1), the words “Except as provided by subsection (c) of this section” are added for clarity. The words “It shall be unlawful for any person” and “upon conviction” are omitted as surplus. The words “fined under title 18” are substituted for “a fine of not more than $15,000” for consistency with title 18. In clause (1), the words “counterfeit” and “falsely make” are omitted as surplus. In clause (4), the words “covering up”, “representation”, and “writing” are omitted as surplus. In clause (7), the word “valid” is omitted as surplus.In subsection (c)(2), before clause (A), the words “fined under title 18” are substituted for “a fine of not more than $25,000” for consistency with title 18.In subsection (d)(1) and (3), the words “Administrator of Drug Enforcement” are substituted for “Drug Enforcement Administration of the Department of Justice” and “Drug Enforcement Administration” because of section 5(a) of Reorganization Plan No. 2 of 1973 (eff. , ). The words “Commissioner of Customs” and “Commissioner” are substituted for “United States Customs Service” because of 19:2071.July 1, 197387 Stat. 1092In subsection (d)(2)(A), the words “aircraft certificate of registration” are substituted for “registration” for consistency in this section. The words “counterfeited” and “falsely made” are omitted as surplus.In subsections (d)(2)(C)(ii) and (e), the words “aircraft certificate of registration” are substituted for “aircraft registration certificate” for consistency with 49 App.:1401, restated in chapter 441 of the revised title.In subsection (e), before clause (1), the words “this subsection or in any other provision of” are omitted as surplus. In clause (1), the words “counterfeits” and “falsely makes” are omitted as surplus. In clause (4)(A), the words “covering up” are omitted as surplus. In clause (4)(B), the words “or representation” are omitted as surplus. In clause (4)(C), the words “writing or” are omitted as surplus.

children.heading: .—Application
children.label: a .—Application
children.text: This section applies only to aircraft not used to provide air transportation.
knowingly and willfully forges or alters a certificate authorized to be issued under this part;
knowingly sells, uses, attempts to use, or possesses with the intent to use, such a certificate;
knowingly and willfully displays or causes to be displayed on an aircraft a mark that is false or misleading about the nationality or registration of the aircraft;

obtains a certificate authorized to be issued under this part by knowingly and willfully falsifying or concealing a material fact, making a false, fictitious, or fraudulent statement, or making or using a false document knowing it contains a false, fictitious, or fraudulent statement or entry;

children.label: A
children.text: the aircraft is not registered under or the certificate of registration is suspended or revoked; orsection 44103 of this title

children.label: B
children.text: the owner knows or has reason to know that the other person does not have proper authorization to operate or navigate the aircraft without registration for a period of time after transfer of ownership;

children.label: A
children.text: the aircraft is not registered under ;section 44103 of this title

children.label: B
children.text: the certificate of registration is suspended or revoked; or

children.label: C
children.text: the person does not have proper authorization to operate or navigate the aircraft without registration for a period of time after transfer of ownership;
knowingly and willfully serves or attempts to serve in any capacity as an airman without an airman’s certificate authorizing the individual to serve in that capacity;
knowingly and willfully employs for service or uses in any capacity as an airman an individual who does not have an airman’s certificate authorizing the individual to serve in that capacity; or

operates an aircraft with a fuel tank or fuel system that has been installed or modified knowing that the tank, system, installation, or modification does not comply with regulations and requirements of the Administrator of the Federal Aviation Administration.
In this subsection, “controlled substance” has the same meaning given that term in section 102 of the Comprehensive Drug Abuse Prevention and Control Act of 1970 ().21 U.S.C. 802

children.label: A
children.text: is punishable by death or imprisonment of more than one year under a law of the United States or a State; or

children.label: B
children.text: that is provided is related to an act punishable by death or imprisonment for more than one year under a law of the United States or a State related to a controlled substance (except a law related to simple possession of a controlled substance).
A term of imprisonment imposed under paragraph (2) of this subsection shall be served in addition to, and not concurrently with, any other term of imprisonment imposed on the individual.

The Administrator of Drug Enforcement or the Commissioner of U.S. Customs and Border Protection may seize and forfeit under the customs laws an aircraft whose use is related to a violation of subsection (b) of this section, or to aid or facilitate a violation, regardless of whether a person is charged with the violation.

children.label: A
children.text: subsection (b)(1) of this section if the aircraft certificate of registration has been forged or altered;

children.label: B
children.text: subsection (b)(3) of this section if there is an external display of false or misleading registration numbers or country of registration;

children.label: i
children.text: the aircraft is registered to a false or fictitious person; or

children.label: ii
children.text: the application form used to obtain the aircraft certificate of registration contains a material false statement;

children.label: D
children.text: subsection (b)(5) of this section if the aircraft was operated when it was not registered under ; orsection 44103 of this title

children.label: i
children.text: in violation of a regulation or requirement of the Administrator of the Federal Aviation Administration; or

children.label: ii
children.text: if a certificate required to be issued for the installation or alteration is not carried on the aircraft.

The Administrator of the Federal Aviation Administration, the Administrator of Drug Enforcement, and the Commissioner shall agree to a memorandum of understanding to establish procedures to carry out this subsection.
knowingly and willfully forges or alters an aircraft certificate of registration;
knowingly sells, uses, attempts to use, or possesses with the intent to use, a fraudulent aircraft certificate of registration;
knowingly and willfully displays or causes to be displayed on an aircraft a mark that is false or misleading about the nationality or registration of the aircraft; or

children.label: A
children.text: knowingly and willfully falsifying or concealing a material fact;

children.label: B
children.text: making a false, fictitious, or fraudulent statement; or

children.label: C
children.text: making or using a false document knowing it contains a false, fictitious, or fraudulent statement or entry.
